title: Quadlet tests: enable device.volume and remove socketactivated.container
body:
These files

* _test/e2e/quadlet/device.volume_
* _test/e2e/quadlet/device-copy.volume_

were added in
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17217/files

but only the test for  _test/e2e/quadlet/device-copy.volume_
was enabled.

I assume that was a mistake. Let's enable 
_test/e2e/quadlet/device.volume_
too.

Remove unused file
_test/e2e/quadlet/socketactivated.container_

comments.body:
The directive `SocketActivated` can be found here
https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Acontainers%2Fquadlet%20socketactivated&type=code

I guess it was removed when quadlet was moved into Podman because socket activation of containers works without it.


A test on Fedora CoreOS 38.20230514.1.0

```
[test@localhost ~]$ rpm -q podman
podman-4.5.0-1.fc38.aarch64
[test@localhost ~]$ cat ~/.config/systemd/user/echo.socket 
[Unit]
Description=echo server
[Socket]
ListenStream=127.0.0.1:3000
[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
[test@localhost ~]$ cat ~/.config/containers/systemd/echo.container 
[Container]
Image=ghcr.io/eriksjolund/socket-activate-echo
[test@localhost ~]$ systemctl --user start echo.socket
[test@localhost ~]$ echo hello | socat - tcp4:127.0.0.1:3000
hello
[test@localhost ~]$ 
```
The test code additions LGTM, I'll let others decide on the removal.
